methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $41M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

data.narrative:
First Resonance is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $40.8M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 private and 1 public company operating in the same sector and stage cohort, including peers such as 1X Technologies ($10.0B, Series B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B, Series B), Dexterity ($1.6B, Series B), Collaborative Robotics ($500M, Series B), and Fairmat ($350M, Series B).

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the $41M total raised as a baseline and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A progression, yielding $82M. The peer-median method identifies six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables with a median valuation of $1.1B and a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. This wide dispersion reflects material heterogeneity in the robotics subsector.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $69M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $578M. The substantial gap between the stage-progression result and peer benchmarks reflects early-stage capital efficiency relative to later-stage valuations in the robotics space. Key valuation uncertainties include company-specific technology differentiation, revenue traction relative to peers, and the applicability of Series B comps to a current Series A position. A more precise valuation would require clarity on First Resonance's revenue scale and growth trajectory relative to the peer set.
